context: Tests whether the agent produces a PlantUML use case diagram from a requirements document using the exact prescribed template format, follows all use case diagram conventions (UC-{3-digit} IDs, FR traceability, actor connectivity), and avoids prohibited patterns (non-standard syntax, implementation details in names).

checklist.name: @startuml / @enduml
checklist.description: The file starts with '@startuml' and ends with '@enduml'

checklist.name: Left to right direction
checklist.description: The file contains the line 'left to right direction'

checklist.name: Actor declaration syntax
checklist.description: All actors are declared using 'actor "Name" as alias' syntax (not plain 'actor Name')

checklist.name: Rectangle system block
checklist.description: Use cases are wrapped inside a 'rectangle "..." { ... }' block

checklist.name: UC-{3-digit} IDs
checklist.description: Every use case alias and label follows the UC-{3-digit} convention (e.g. UC-001, UC-002)

checklist.name: Usecase two-line format
checklist.description: Use cases are declared using 'usecase "UC-XXX\nDescription" as UCxxx' with the ID and description on separate lines within the string

checklist.name: Arrow syntax
checklist.description: Connections between actors and use cases use '-->' arrows

checklist.name: All actors connected
checklist.description: Every declared actor is connected to at least one use case via a '-->' arrow

checklist.name: FR coverage (no NFR/constraint use cases)
checklist.description: The number of use cases roughly matches the number of functional requirements (8), and does NOT include use cases derived from non-functional requirements (NFR-001 to NFR-004) or constraints (C-001 to C-003)

checklist.name: No implementation details in names
checklist.description: Use case labels do NOT contain technical identifiers such as HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), controller names, class names, or SQL statements
